text.title: Technology Stack Selection Decision Tree Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: ADR picks a new language because the architect wants to learn it; team has no expertise.
antipattern.root-cause: r1 ignored; personal-learning goal smuggled into a team-impact decision.
antipattern.fix: Block ADRs where team familiarity score < 0.5 unless there is a documented training budget + hiring plan + senior hire in role.

antipattern.symptom: Datastore picked because 'it is the most popular this year' (e.g. Mongo for relational data).
antipattern.root-cause: r4 skipped; data shape never modelled.
antipattern.fix: Require a data-shape diagram (entities + access patterns) before the datastore row is filled.

antipattern.symptom: Pre-1.0 framework picked without a risk budget; team burns 3 sprints on upstream bugs.
antipattern.fix: Mandate a 'risk_budget_weeks' field in the ADR for any framework < 2 years GA; reject if empty.

antipattern.symptom: Stack picked years ago; hiring is failing; nobody re-evaluates.
antipattern.root-cause: r5 skipped; ADR has no review trigger.
antipattern.fix: Require review_trigger field; calendar reminder created at merge.
